Use of a Representative (IMM 5476)

sections.heading: Sending your application online through Connect
items: We use Canada Post’s Connect service to manage your application online. This service allows for electronic file transfers that are secure, private, and confidential. Also, the service is free for you.
You can use Connect to:
When you ask to manage your application online, you’re consenting to communicate with us using Connect about all parts of your PRRA application.
You can ask to start managing your application online at any time during the process until a decision has been made.
To start using Connect, sign up through our Humanitarian Migration Office by following these steps:
Ask to start using the service by submitting a Canada Post Connect Sign-up Request . You will be asked to provide :
You’ll get an email the next business day from Canada Post asking you to register online.
Follow the instructions in the email or see the Canada Post Connect Frequently Asked Questions .
Submit your documents using your Connect account before they are due.
To submit them through Connect, they must:
Refrain from adding any text in the Message box. All the information you wish to provide to IRCC must be included in the documents you attach to the message.
The Sent time in the message indicates that your document(s) have been received.
Ensure to save your original document(s) on your computer or network as Connect is not meant for saving your files.

Once you have signed up with our Humanitarian Migration office to communicate with us online regarding your PRRA application, we will contact your through Connect if you need a PRRA hearing or if we need further information or documentation. When we contact you through Connect, the Sent time in the message indicates that you have received our document(s).
To submit more documents at a later date, just log into Connect and submit your new documents.
bullets: send us your application and supporting documents
get information about your PRRA hearing, if you need one
send additional information when we ask for it
update your application and personal information if your circumstances change
your full name as it appears on your passport or official documents (don’t use initials)
your Unique Client Identifier (UCI) or Client Identification (ID)
the email address you want to use with Connect
be submitted in accordance with this Guide (see Overview , Before You Apply and How to complete the form sections)
should not exceed 25 Megabytes (MB) if your documents exceed 25 MB, split them into separate attachments that do not exceed 25 MB each and send them in separate messages
if your documents exceed 25 MB, split them into separate attachments that do not exceed 25 MB each and send them in separate messages
be in PDF format (please convert all document files to PDF format prior to submission) for documents requiring a signature, you must scan and submit the signed document
for documents requiring a signature, you must scan and submit the signed document

include sufficient information about you to allow our Humanitarian Migration office to match the documents you’re sending to your PRRA file, such as: your Unique Client Identifier (UCI) or Client Identification (ID), your full name as it appears on your passport or official documents (don’t use initials), and your date of birth

sections.heading: Mailing your application
items: Use a 23 cm x 30.5 cm (9” x 12”) envelope. Address the envelope as follows

sections.heading: May I work while my application is being processed?
Protecting your information
items: Your personal information is:
For more information about the protection of your data, visit the Help Centre .
bullets: available to IRCC and the Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA) employees who need to see it to provide the services to you, and
not disclosed to other organizations except as permitted under the provisions of the Privacy Act or the Immigration and Refugee Protection Regulations .

sections.heading: Need help?
items: If you need help, you can find answers to your questions by visiting the Help Centre .
